Question: Find the x- and y -intercepts for the following: $$2 x - 5 y = 6$$
Find the x- and y -intercepts of this equation, and sketch the graph of the line using just the intercepts plotted. $$3 x - 4 y = 12$$
Find the exact distance between $$( 5 , -3 )$$ and $$( - 2 , 8 ) .$$ Find the coordinates of the midpoint of the line segment joining the two points.
Write the interval notation for the set of numbers represented by $$\{ x | x \le 9 \} .$$
The perimeter of a triangle is 30 in. The longest side is 2 less than 3 times the shortest side and the other side is 2 more than twice the shortest side. Find the length of each side.
Solve for x . Write the answer in simplest radical form. $$\frac{x^{2}}{3} - x = - \frac{1}{2}$$
